name: songbird-collection
description: A custom marketplace of Claude Code plugins for both experienced developers and vibe coders, extending Claude Code with workflows, commands, and integrations that fit how you actually build.

plugins.name: jetbrains-router
plugins.description: Makes Claude read and edit files through your JetBrains IDE rather than directly from disk. This gives Claude your IDE's error detection, smarter file searches, and visibility into changes you haven't saved yet. Still works if the IDE isn't running. Requires JetBrains 2025.2+ with MCP Server enabled.

plugins.name: forge
plugins.description: Investigates your codebase before you build. Parallel domain experts surface integration points and risks, an adversarial critic stress-tests the plan against real code — nothing gets implemented without your sign-off.

plugins.name: nudge
plugins.description: Reviews your session and teaches you how to write better prompts. Run it at the end of any working session — it identifies what your opening prompt missed, rewrites it as a professional prompt calibrated to your Claude model and effort level, and explains why in plain language.

plugins.name: verity
plugins.description: Fetches current official Claude Code documentation on demand from code.claude.com — gives Claude truth-grounding from primary sources instead of training memory. Also provides a canonical reference for undocumented host MCP tools (spawn_task, mark_chapter, show_widget, and the full ccd_session and visualize families).

plugins.name: foreman
plugins.description: Prompt-engineering and project-roadmap plugin for Claude Code. /foreman:craft-prompt assembles self-contained, Anthropic-grade prompts via AskUserQuestion. /foreman:init bootstraps a project's ROADMAP.jsonl and Claude-suggestion policy. /foreman:roadmap picks the next task like a software architect and crafts its handoff prompt. /foreman:survey ground-truths the near-term candidates against the actual codebase, on demand, and persists findings back into the roadmap. A commit-triggered hook keeps roadmap status in sync and, opt-in, asks about newly-discovered opportunities; a second hook blocks direct ROADMAP.jsonl edits, forcing changes through the CLI; a third reruns Foreman's own test suite after an edit to its scripts/hooks and flags a regression immediately.
